selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is -4.3% and 20-day momentum is 11.0%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 5.9% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 4.7%, downside volatility near 4.0%, and a recent max drawdown of 12.9%.
Volume confirmation is 28.4% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is -2.7% versus SPY over 5 days and -0.3% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.86, confidence 0.82, and 0.8 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.86, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 2 sources, 0.8 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.88, average source quality 0.77, confidence 0.82.
Broad market news leaned bearish for the Technology sector with score 0.41 and confidence 0.38.

ASML's pullback reflects a short-lived sentiment reversal in chip/AI stocks. First-order effect: lower near-term valuation and momentum for semiconductor-equipment names as investors pause on capex expectations. Second-order effect: transient pressure on broader technology/AI hardware suppliers and related semiconductor suppliers if investors broadly mark down near-term demand. Impact is likely short-lived (days) unless the pause signals a sustained change in capex guidance or order flows; no specific company within tracked symbols is directly implicated by the article.
Sector overlay was trimmed by 35% because it overlaps with company or macro themes.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.065 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Oracle (ORCL) – Among the 10 No-Brainer Dividend Stocks to Buy
news_evidence.llm_reason: Contains an analyst price-target raise (Scotiabank to $290) and reiteration of Outperform plus positive commentary on Oracle’s AI-accelerated cloud position — news that can move sentiment and shares in the near term.

news_evidence.llm_reason: Dedicated buy/hold/sell analysis of Oracle, referencing the latest quarter, large contracted backlog and reframing of its AI infrastructure strategy — material fundamental and sentiment information that can influence near-term trading.
